Re: Request for coufirDatiou of TelephoDo Aalvice
Dear Jeanne:
Last month, I telephoned you regarding the 1991 campaign
filing 
respons ibil ities of our client, Daniel P. Levin. our
inquiry on his behaLf was prornpted by a phone call Mr. Levin
received from the Redwood City Clerk advising hin a campaign
filing might be due by tlarch 31,. The city Clerk asked that he
review his situation with the FPPC.
I advised you that t1r. Levin rnade independent expenditures
in l-989 and 1990 in support of the gualification of a Redwood
city initiative 
measure which was titled by the Redl^rood city city
Attorney as 'rCity owned waterfront land sale proposed initiative
neasure (Bayport)'t. This initiative 
was titled and the petitions
were circulated between November, 1989 and April, 1990. He
turned in sufficient signatures (over 1,0,000) to the City clerk
to qualify the neasure for the ballot. 
The city initiated legal
action to keep the measure off the balLot. The court took the
initiative 
off the ballot, ruting that the i,nitiative, 
as
drafted, contained constitutional and other deficiencies.
election on Mr. Levints initiative 
$ras ever he1d. 
1
1 Thereafter, in July 1990, Mr. Levin submitted two
initiative 
measures to the Red$/ood city city Attorney for
titling. 
one received a title 
and sunmaryi once again }egal
action was commenced to keep the second measure off the ballot on
grounds similar to the prior legal action. 
The l"awsuit was
successful. Since the initiative 
that did receive a title 
and
summary r,/as a companion to the initiative 
inval-idated by the
court, nej,ther was ever placed in circutation or subrnitted to the
city c1erk. Hence, it is our understanding that no Independent
Expenditure or Major Donor filings erere required rel-ative to the
two JuIy initiatives.
No


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
lilay 10 , 19 9 1
Page 2
with our f irmts advice and assistance, Mr. Levin filed an
Independent Expenditure and lIajor Donor Carrpaign Statement (FppC
Form 461) covering the period January L, l-989 through I.Iay 2,
1990, ("post subrnission'r deadline) ; ltay 3, L99O through June 30,
1-990 (semi annual) ; and July 1,, L990 through December 31, t-990,
(semi annual) . Since llr. Levinrs last filing, 
no expenditures
have been nade j-n connection with the measure which qualified for
the ballot but then was removed through court action or the two
measures subnitted in JuIy, 1990.
In L99l-, the only expenditures made by llr. Levin have been
to seek lega1 counsel on possible language for ordinances which,
if adopted, hrould acconplish the same effect as the initiative.
Ho$/ever, no initiative 
has been submitted to the city for title
and sunmary at this time. There has been no attempt to influence
the voters in connection with any measure.
In my telephone conversation with you, we agreed that I,1r.
Levin's current activity is not an independent expenditure as
that term is defined in Government Code Section 82031 and there
i-s no rrneasuretr before the voters as discussed in the Fontana
opinion (2 FPPC Opinions 25, 2/4/76).
We agreed that based on the above facts, Mr. Levin has no
filing responsibil it ies as an independent expenditure filer with
the city of Red\"rood city. 
I r.voutd appreciate your conf irmation
of this advice in writing.
If you have any questions or I have rnisstated any of our
discussions, please do not hesitate to contact me.
KAP/cn
